title: mucuna keyensis
intro: Mucuna keyensis, also known as the Mucuna pruriens var. keyensis, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family. It is a tropical plant that contains L-DOPA, an amino acid that serves as a precursor to neurotransmitters such as dopamine, serotonin, and norepinephrine. The medicinal properties of Mucuna keyensis include its ability to improve cognitive function, enhance mood, and alleviate symptoms of Parkinson's disease and other neurodegenerative disorders, due to its high L-DOPA content. The plant is commonly prepared as a powder or extract for herbal use, and can be consumed in the form of capsules, teas, or added to food products.
